steps.title: Identify the exceptional finding
steps.content: The passage first explains the traveling wave rule: when steepness passes a critical threshold, breaking occurs and constrains wave height. Then the decisive clue: spike waves can exceed that constraint. This violation of the expected rule is the passage's main point.

steps.title: Match the finding to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C states that spike waves “may be higher than would be expected based on the properties of traveling waves”—which is exactly what exceeding a constraint means.

The other choices either oversimplify (A), suggest steepness still limits spike waves (B), or focus on traveling waves (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
